hotels.name: Hyatt Regency McCormick Place
hotels.address: 2233 S Martin Luther King Dr, Chicago, IL 60616

activities.description: Attend business conference sessions and networking events
Classic Chicago deep-dish pizza near hotel
activities.location: Lou Malnati's - South Loop (805 S State St)
activities.notes: Try the classic deep-dish with sausage

activities.location: Giordano's - South Loop (1340 S Michigan Ave)

activities.activity: Visit The Art Institute of Chicago
activities.description: Explore one of the world's greatest art museums, featuring works from Monet, Van Gogh, Picasso, and American Gothic
activities.notes: Don't miss the Modern Wing and Impressionist collection

activities.description: Final deep-dish pizza experience - famous for caramelized crust
activities.location: Pequod's Pizza - Lincoln Park (2207 N Clybourn Ave)
activities.notes: Order the pan pizza with caramelized cheese crust

activities.activity: Return to hotel and depart for airport
activities.description: Check out, travel to O'Hare for evening flight
activities.notes: Allow extra time for evening traffic

plan.reasoning: This itinerary perfectly meets all requirements: (1) Hyatt Regency McCormick Place is directly connected to the convention center via skybridge (under 10-minute drive/walk) and costs $219/night, well under the $250 budget, with excellent WiFi and a full business center. (2) Flights from NYC arrive early on Sept 15 and depart evening of Sept 17, covering the conference dates (Sept 15-16) plus the extra day. (3) The extra day (Sept 17) includes a visit to the Art Institute of Chicago and multiple deep-dish pizza experiences throughout the trip at iconic Chicago establishments (Lou Malnati's, Giordano's, and Pequod's). (4) Total cost of $1,103 includes flights ($483), hotel ($438), activities/admission ($32), meals ($105), and transportation ($45). The hotel location is ideal for the business traveler, and the itinerary balances conference obligations with authentic Chicago experiences.
plan.final_message_to_user: I've created a complete 3-day Chicago itinerary for your conference trip! You'll stay at the Hyatt Regency McCormick Place ($219/night), which is directly connected to the convention center via skybridge and features excellent WiFi and a business center. Your flights arrive early on the 15th and depart evening of the 17th, giving you time for the full conference plus your extra day. I've included visits to three iconic deep-dish pizza spots (Lou Malnati's, Giordano's, and Pequod's) and a morning at the Art Institute on your free day. Total estimated cost: $1,103. Have a productive conference and enjoy Chicago!
